extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE won from a quality front group after GUERREIRO's attack on the final climb splintered the race, outsprinting ROGLIČ again.
tactical_patterns: Astana burned matches driving chase of 11-rider break but had no finisher when GUERREIRO attacked final climb.
MAS Enric's attack sheltered ALAPHILIPPE before ROGLIČ response; Quick-Step using young rider leader as domestique tactically.
MATTHEWS Michael survived into final climb selection despite being pure sprinter, signaling weakness in final ascent's difficulty.
BUCHMANN and KONRAD both finishing strongly suggests Bora sending dual climber threats on selective finishes.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: consecutive stage wins confirms peak form and ability to outsprint climbers after selective climbs
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: second again to ALAPHILIPPE suggests slight sprint deficit but matching climbing ability
rider_signals.GUERREIRO Ruben: positive: attack on final climb created selection, showing aggressive form beyond expected level
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: working for ALAPHILIPPE despite holding young rider jersey reveals team confidence in his condition
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: made front selection with IZAGIRRE Gorka as dual Movistar threat on steep finale
rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Gorka: positive: both IZAGIRRE brothers stronger on second day, suggesting slow start to stage race
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: strong finish with KONRAD suggests Bora deploying multiple protected riders on hard finales
rider_signals.KONRAD Patrick: positive: bridged to lead group late, indicating climbing form superior to GC positioning suggests
rider_signals.PRADES Eduard: positive: sixth place surprise finish reveals climbing development beyond expectations
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: negative: disappointing result after Astana's chase work indicates form below team investment
